**Project Title: AURIA\
Owner Name(s): Wenqing (responsible), Silja, Sara, Inga-Maria\
Start Date: 2024\
Expected Completion Date: 2025**

**đź§·This project is a collaboration with Sorger lab, all updates are in
Dropbox [[Auria_TMA --
Dropbox]{.underline}](https://www.dropbox.com/home/Auria_TMA)**

**GitHub Repository (if applicable):**

# **Project Overview**

Oxidative stress is an important factor in reprogramming tumor cells and
the tumor microenvironment. Triple-negative breast cancer is a highly
aggressive and heterogeneous disease lacking targeted therapies and
showing an overall poor response to immunotherapy. An increased

ment. Triple-negative breast cancer is a highly
aggressive and heterogeneous disease lacking targeted therapies and
showing an overall poor response to immunotherapy. An increased
understanding of the complex interplay of oxidative stress and hypoxia
in tumor cell-intrinsic and extrinsic features is required for the
development of new treatment strategies. The aim is to identify markers
for oxidative stress and hypoxia in TNBC, and these metabolically
distinct environments contain different immune cell populations. This is
assessed in CyCIF, scRNAseq, and Visium data.

## **Input Files**

scRNAseq:

- [[https://www.nature.com/articles/s41588-021-00911-1]{.underline}](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41588-021-00911-1)

TMA(Data from Inga-Maria):

- PDrive /h345/afarkkilab/Data/17_Auria_TMA

## **Methods**

### **UCell**

- [[[https://github.com/carmonalab/UCell]{.underline}]{.mark}](https://github.com/carmonalab/UCell)

- [An R package for scoring gene signatures in single-cell
 > datasets.]{.mark}

### **SpottedPy**

- [[https://github.com/secrierlab/SpottedPy]{.underline}](https://github.com/secrierlab/SpottedPy)

- [A Python package for analyzing signatures in spatial transcriptomic
 > datasets at varying scales using hotspot (spatial cluster)
 > analysis and neighborhood enrichment.]{.mark}

## **Results**

- The results are mainly generated on the go.

<!-- -->

## **Results**

- The results are mainly generated on the go.

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
- Preliminary results generated by all members are in Dropbox.

### **Conclusions**

- Within TNBC tumors, there is no single tumor cell population
 > expressing multiple antioxidants.

- Individual antioxidants are differently expressed across tumor zones
 > and microenvironments.

- GCLC+VIM+ tumor cells are spatially associated with immune cells.
 > They may predict better patient outcomes because of their
 > increased sensitivity to chemotherapy (markers and scRNA-seq
 > suggest a healthy, potentially proliferative cell state).

## **Week 14**

### **Summary of this period**

- Basic information about the tCyCIF samples

 - **Raw data:** It means all images

 - \[\'19_02_02A\', \'M1\', \'19_02_01A\', \'T2\', \'INF2\',
 > \'19_02_03A\', \'1B\', \'2B\', \'3B\', \'INF1\', \'INF3\',
 > \'T3\', \'T1\'\]

 - **Filtered data:**

 - Remove some TMA images (the red samples), because some
 > functional markers are not work

 - But here both of them are **scaled data (Scimap gated and
 > rescaled)**

![](media/image7.png){width="5.551619641294838in"
height="3.894576771653543in"}

- Celltype proportion by RCNs

 - Scimap Spatial count with radius = 45 px (30 microns) and
 > k-means = 10

 - Named these 10 clusters as RCNs

![](media/image5.png){width="6.520875984251968in"
height="4.321976159230096in"}

- Antioxidant markers expression within RCNs

 - Antioxidant markers (GCLC, NQO1, TXNRD1)

 - GCLC has higher expression in RCN4 (VIM+.stroma), RCN6
 > (CD4.T.cells), RCN8 (CD206.Macrophages), and RCN9
 > (CD26.Macrophages) rich neighborhoods.

![](media/image3.png){width="6.732136920384952in"
height="3.4418318022747156in"}

- Antioxidant markers expression within different celltype_annotation

 - Antioxidant markers are concentrated in the inflammation

2136920384952in"
height="3.4418318022747156in"}

- Antioxidant markers expression within different celltype_annotation

 - Antioxidant markers are concentrated in the inflammation
 > locations.

 - In other areas, GCLC has high expression in immune cells.

![](media/image4.png){width="4.124524278215223in"
height="4.321919291338583in"}

- In tumor cells, GCLC has higher correlation with Vimentin.
